title: Sketching Signals
summary: This page explains the concept of the time constant for decaying exponential signals, defining it as the time interval over which the exponential drops to 1/e (approximately 37%) of its value. It demonstrates how to use the time constant to quickly sketch exponential functions, using e^{-2t} as a worked example with a time constant of 0.5 seconds. Figures illustrate both general monotonic exponentials and specific sketches of e^{-at} and e^{-2t}.

figures.caption: Sketching (a) e^{-at}u(t) and (b) e^{-2t}u(t), illustrating the time constant and key amplitude values at 0.37 and 0.135.
